ddls:
CREATE TABLE "test_items" (
  "test_item_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test item */,
  "test_item_name" TEXT /* Name of the test item */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the test item */,
  "standard_id" INTEGER /* ID of the standard used for the test */,
  "test_protocol" TEXT /* Testing protocol (e.g., HTGB, HTRB) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("test_item_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_conditions" (
  "test_condition_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test condition */,
  "test_condition_name" TEXT /* Name of the test condition */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the test condition */,
  "temperature" REAL /* Temperature value */,
  "voltage" REAL /* Voltage value */,
  "humidity" REAL /* Humidity value (if applicable) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("test_condition_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "time_points" (
  "time_point_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each time point */,
  "time_point_name" TEXT /* Name of the time point */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the time point */,
  "duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the time point in hours or minutes */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("time_point_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_results" (
  "test_result_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test result */,
  "test_item_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test item */,
  "test_condition_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test condition */,
  "time_point_id" INTEGER /* ID of the time point */,
  "total_sample_size" INTEGER /* Total sample size for the test */,
  "number_of_failures" INTEGER /* Number of failures for the test */,
  "standard_id" INTEGER /* ID of the standard used for the test */,
  "test_protocol" TEXT /* Testing protocol (e.g., HTGB, HTRB) */,
  "test_environment" TEXT /* Description of the test environment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("test_result_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_results_standard_id FOREIGN KEY ("standard_id") REFERENCES standards ("standard_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_results_test_condition_id FOREIGN KEY ("test_condition_id") REFERENCES test_conditions ("test_condition_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_results_time_point_id FOREIGN KEY ("time_point_id") REFERENCES time_points ("time_point_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_results_test_item_id FOREIGN KEY ("test_item_id") REFERENCES test_items ("test_item_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "standards" (
  "standard_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each standard */,
  "standard_name" TEXT /* Name of the standard */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the standard */,
  "protocol" TEXT /* Testing protocol associated with the standard */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version of the standard */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("standard_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_cycles" (
  "test_cycle_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test cycle */,
  "test_result_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test result */,
  "cycle_number" INTEGER /* Cycle number */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the cycle */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the cycle */,
  "test_environment" TEXT /* Description of the test environment */,
  "comments" TEXT /* Additional comments or notes for the cycle */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("test_cycle_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_cycles_test_result_id FOREIGN KEY ("test_result_id") REFERENCES test_results ("test_result_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_schedules" (
  "test_schedule_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test schedule */,
  "test_item_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test item */,
  "test_condition_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test condition */,
  "time_point_id" INTEGER /* ID of the time point */,
  "schedule_date" TEXT /* Schedule date for the test */,
  "test_environment" TEXT /* Description of the test environment */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the schedule (e.g., pending, in progress, completed) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("test_schedule_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_schedules_test_item_id FOREIGN KEY ("test_item_id") REFERENCES test_items ("test_item_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_schedules_time_point_id FOREIGN KEY ("time_point_id") REFERENCES time_points ("time_point_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_schedules_test_condition_id FOREIGN KEY ("test_condition_id") REFERENCES test_conditions ("test_condition_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_reminders" (
  "test_reminder_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test reminder */,
  "test_schedule_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test schedule */,
  "reminder_date" TEXT /* Date of the reminder */,
  "reminder_time" TEXT /* Time of the reminder */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the reminder (e.g., pending, sent, acknowledged) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("test_reminder_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_reminders_test_schedule_id FOREIGN KEY ("test_schedule_id") REFERENCES test_schedules ("test_schedule_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_completion" (
  "test_completion_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test completion */,
  "test_result_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test result */,
  "completion_date" TEXT /* Date of completion */,
  "completion_time" TEXT /* Time of completion */,
  "test_environment" TEXT /* Description of the test environment */,
  "comments" TEXT /* Additional comments or notes for the completion */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("test_completion_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_completion_test_result_id FOREIGN KEY ("test_result_id") REFERENCES test_results ("test_result_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "inventory" (
  "inventory_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each inventory item */,
  "component_id" INTEGER /* ID of the component */,
  "quantity" INTEGER /* Quantity of the component in inventory */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the inventory (e.g., warehouse, lab) */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the inventory (e.g., available, in use, reserved) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("inventory_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_inventory_component_id FOREIGN KEY ("component_id") REFERENCES components ("component_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "components" (
  "component_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each component */,
  "component_name" TEXT /* Name of the component */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the component */,
  "manufactured_date" TEXT /* Date of manufacture */,
  "manufacturer" TEXT /* Manufacturer of the component */,
  "test_status" TEXT /* Status of the component in testing (e.g., not tested, testing, passed, failed) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("component_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., test engineer, quality control manager, admin) */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user (e.g., engineering, quality control) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "user_role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user role */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user */,
  "permission" TEXT /* Permission granted to the user */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user (e.g., engineering, quality control) */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last login date and time */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_item_components" (
  "test_item_component_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test item component relationship */,
  "test_item_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test item */,
  "component_id" INTEGER /* ID of the component */,
  "quantity_used" INTEGER /* Quantity of the component used in the test */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("test_item_component_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_item_components_test_item_id FOREIGN KEY ("test_item_id") REFERENCES test_items ("test_item_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_item_components_component_id FOREIGN KEY ("component_id") REFERENCES components ("component_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_item_inventories" (
  "test_item_inventory_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test item inventory relationship */,
  "test_item_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test item */,
  "inventory_id" INTEGER /* ID of the inventory item */,
  "quantity_allocated" INTEGER /* Quantity of the inventory item allocated to the test */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("test_item_inventory_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_item_inventories_test_item_id FOREIGN KEY ("test_item_id") REFERENCES test_items ("test_item_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_item_inventories_inventory_id FOREIGN KEY ("inventory_id") REFERENCES inventory ("inventory_id")
)
